destinations.evidence: The media variants are included in the executor-capability predicate.

equivalence_argument: The TEE registry reader treats ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall as executor capabilities through the shared is_executor_capability predicate used when retaining executor records.
notes: The capability classification is local to the port registry-reader crate and is consumed by RPC and verifier paths.
rationale: The primary diff extends Capability::try_from for values 7, 8, and 9 and includes the new multimodal variants in is_executor_capability. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The TEE registry reader should treat ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall capabilities as executor-service capabilities for registration and validation purposes.
suggested_falsification_tests: Assert Capability::try_from(7/8/9) returns ImageCall/AudioCall/VideoCall and that is_executor_capability returns true for each; assert unrelated service capabilities retain their prior behavior.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Assert Capability::try_from(7/8/9) returns ImageCall/AudioCall/VideoCall and that is_executor_capability returns true for each; assert unrelated service capabilities retain their prior behavior.
title: Multimodal capabilities are executor capabilities
